methods.description: Integration of the Community Atmosphere Model version 3.1 (CAM 3.1) with the Community Land Model version 3 (CLM3), a thermodynamic sea ice model, and a data ocean to simulate energy fluxes and climate features.

steps.description: Integrate CAM 3.1 with CLM3, a thermodynamic sea ice model, and a data ocean.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate CAM 3.1 with CLM3 and sea ice model

steps.description: Use the thermodynamic sea ice model derived from the Community Sea Ice Model version 5 to calculate energy fluxes between the ice and the overlying atmosphere.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate energy fluxes using sea ice model

steps.description: Employ a T42 spectral truncation with 26 vertical levels in CAM3.1, corresponding to a horizontal resolution of approximately 2.8° latitude × 2.8° longitude.
steps.searchable_summary: set CAM3.1 resolution to T42 spectral truncation

steps.description: Implement prognostic cloud water parameterization and new radiative parameterizations in CAM3.1.
steps.searchable_summary: implement cloud water and radiative parameterizations

steps.description: Replace uniform background aerosol with present-day climatology for shortwave flux calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: use present-day climatology for shortwave flux

steps.description: Formulate vertical diffusion of dry static energy and separate physics and dynamics in CAM3.1.
steps.searchable_summary: formulate vertical diffusion and separate physics/dynamics
